1. Non-ST-Elevation Myocardial Infarction (NSTEMI) — Elevated troponin with ischemic ECG changes (ST depression/T-wave inversion) and classic anginal symptoms in a high-risk patient.
2. Unstable Angina — Ischemic chest pain at rest with dynamic ECG changes, though the elevated troponin technically classifies this as NSTEMI rather than pure unstable angina.
3. Aortic Dissection — Acute tearing chest pain with hypertension and risk factors, but less likely due to the absence of widened mediastinum on CXR and lack of pulse deficits.
4. Pulmonary Embolism — Dyspnea and tachycardia are present, but
label: macmini-m4-32gb.rapidmlx.qwen3.6-35b.4bit-mlx.ddx_en
